reviews.review_text:
Cyberpunk 2077 isn’t just a game.
And Edgerunners isn’t just an anime.

Both present a futuristic world full of chrome, violence, and massive corporations… but at its core, it reflects something very real: real life.

Today, people often burn themselves out chasing ambition, money, status, or validation.
In Night City, it happens through cyberware and implants.
In real life, it happens in quieter, less neon-lit ways—but it still destroys people just the same.

David Martinez wanted to prove his worth to the world.
V just wanted to survive in a system that was already stacked against them.
And honestly, how many people today are living versions of that same struggle?

Cyberpunk constantly reminds us that when ambition goes blind, it stops motivating and starts consuming you instead.
People stop living for themselves and start chasing something that may never fill the emptiness.

And that’s what makes this universe so special:
behind the neon lights, intense action, and the absolutely unhinged amount of chaos (and yes, NSFW energy) in Night City, there are real lessons about humanity, loneliness, love, trauma, and the price of trying to “win” in a broken world.

There are no perfect endings in Night City.
Just like there are rarely perfect endings in real life.

But people still love, still dream, and still fight anyway.

And maybe that’s the real message of Cyberpunk.

(Also, half the city definitely needs therapy… and the other half needs to log off for a bit.)

10/10

[h1] CHOOM, THIS IS THE MASTERPIECE OF NIGHT CITY [/h1]

[b][i]  Conquered Night City with a flawless 100% completion badge. I didn't just play this game; I lived, chromed-up, and burned this city to the ground alongside Johnny Silverhand.

Let’s be real, its launch was a complete cyber-disaster, but CD Projekt Red turned this ship around so hard that it evolved into an absolute legendary ride. From the tragic, existential depths of the main story to the unmatched, neon-soaked dystopian atmosphere, it is now a pure masterpiece. The massive Phantom Liberty DLC takes the experience to an entirely new level, dragging you into the gritty, high-stakes spy thriller world of Dogtown with an unforgettable narrative.

But amidst all the chaos, betrayal, and chrome, true love found its way in the badlands. Finding real connection with Panam Palmer, fighting alongside the Aldecaldos, and ending this journey with her by my side was the ultimate peak of this story. If you haven't achieved everything and driven into the sunset with Panam, you haven't truly seen Night City. A masterpiece that owns a permanent crown on my profile.

Wake the f**k up, Samurai. We have a city to burn. 9/10  [/i][/b]

Cyberpunk 2077 had a rocky release due to the premature release, but it eventually redeemed itself with the release of Phantom Liberty along with several quality of life improvements. Now, it would be safe to say that Cyberpunk 2077 is in a playable state, although not perfect by any means. It is still possible run into some bugs, and the AI still needs adjustments, especially for the pedestrians. They simply jump in front of the car, causing NCPD to tail you unnecessarily.

However, they’re not too big of a problem to take you out of the experience. Night City looks astonishing, the combat feels great, and the story pulls you in just fine. Can’t forget about the amazing soundtrack as well. All in-game soundtrack and the radio stations are simply bangers that they got me hooked the day I started playing it.

As I’ve mentioned before, the game used to be a wreck, therefore; I purposely waited for them to sort out the kinks before playing, and I don’t regret that decision. It was marketed as an open world RPG initially and I believe it still qualifies as one despite many claim it isn’t. There’s an in-depth character customization, and many skill trees to choose from as well as implants that can personalize your experience according to your tastes and needs.

Depending on which skill tree you chose to upgrade, the gameplay changes drastically. They can even be combined with others to create a hybrid build. Furthermore, Phantom Liberty adds the additional Relic Tree to flesh out the already existing builds. There are simply too many possibilities that it would be unfair to say that there isn’t enough to lead a unique experience.

The story is where the game shines best, as many have already stated before. However, the main missions are mostly focused on world-building, thus they lack enough combat to make you properly appreciate what the game truly offers. Nevertheless, Cyberpunk 2077 isn’t a game that should be breezed through. One should tackle side quests too if they want to unlock the other endings and see the bond between V and other characters.

Johnny Silverhand’s story also gets fleshed out and told mostly through side quests. Many also claim that Keanu Reeves’ involvement ruined what could’ve been a great game otherwise, but I’m inclined to disagree with that take. While it is true that his involvement happened quite late in development and led to many rewrites in the script, I’d say it turned out great.

The only aspect that left me a bit disappointed was the fact that V’s background doesn’t impact that story as much as I had hoped. The only truly different part, depending on your background, is the introduction mission and how you ended up in Night City and met Jackie Welles. All in all, Cyberpunk 2077 is a must-play. You could also watch Edgerunners to get a better grasp on the universe it is based on.

[h1]TL;DR[/h1]
Despite having a rocky headstart, Cyberpunk 2077 managed to pull itself together with Phantom Liberty as well as other patches. It offers a great storyline, strong combat, and a heavily customizable experience. The soundtrack is also noteworthy, and the universe it was built upon feels real in all aspects!

[h1] AMAZING GAME [/h1]

Cyberpunk is exactly my kind of game. FPS + Fast-Paced + Dialogue Choices + Looter-Shooter + RPG + Open World + Futuristic + Romance, it's the absolute ultimate combination. The gunplay is pretty solid, the story is engaging, and the dialogue choices are interesting yet a little bit risky, so you can't just click through them without thinking.

[h2] The World & Loot [/h2]
The characters are interesting, though they could still use a bit more development and deep-diving into their backstories. Still, I love Johnny. He's probably one of the best companions in gaming since Elizabeth from Bioshock Infinite. He’s like an uncle who tells me what to do and warns me whether something is safe or not.

The looter-shooter mechanics are absolutely rewarding af. You don't have to level up or anything just to equip a weapon, which makes exploration feel so much more worthwhile. It’s unlike those other games where you’re like, [i]"Wow, I found this great looking gun after exploring hour after hour, finally!"[/i] only for the game to say, [i]"Congratulations on finding this gun! Now go waste some more time leveling up just to equip it."[/i] Like, bruh~.

Some people wanted this game to be in TPP, but not me. I’m relieved it turned out to be FPP. I play Fallout and Skyrim in first-person too, and even for survival games, I always prefer and enjoy FPP way more.

[h3] My Current Hoard: [/h3]
[*] 150 Weapons
[*] 55 Melee Weapons
[*] 484 Clothing Items
[*] 14 Vehicles
[*] 200+ Quests done in the base game alone (and I still haven't collected everything! :))

---

[h2] Enough for the review now it's yapping time, my journey to playing this game (Cuz it's such a memory....) [/h2]

I remember when this game blew up everywhere. I saw the memes, my favorite YouTuber uploading build videos, and all the complaints about it being a buggy mess in terrible shape. 

I got curious and searched YouTube for the gameplay, and damn, it looked cool af. I realized this was the exact game I had wanted for a long time. I wanted to play it so bad, but I didn't have a device that could run it. Even though it was buggy, I just didn't care. I ended up turning off the video and told myself:

[quote] "I will definitely play this game in the future." [/quote]

I stopped watching because I knew I'd play it eventually, and I didn't want to spoil anything. Time passed, and I kept saving money from school. It was a constant cycle: saving money > building a PC > components failing/faulty > saving money again > building a PC again > failing again > repeat.

Then, in 2024, I finally built a PC with an [b]i5-3470 + RX 460 2GB + 8GB RAM[/b]. An absolute potato, but hey, I could finally play! With the help of some optimization mods, I beat the game and the DLC with 80 hours of playtime. And you know what? My GPU literally died right after I beat it lol. At least I finally got to play it and reached my dream. I always praise this game and recommend it to friends or random people. One of my friends actually loved it and became a huge fan!

[spoiler] RIP to the RX 460 though, it died a hero's death. [/spoiler]
10/10..one of the best games that I have ever played..got everything that you want in a video game..What a journey..PS: <3 JS

So I never played this game at launch and only played it years later. Ngl, I didn't want to play through it at first but after buying it i told myself to play if for at least 3 days. It turned into a 3 month obsession. I didn't know it was a game where you could get different endings. This game was genuinely somewhat responsible for ending a 2 year relationship i had with my fiance because of how real if felt and how nihilistic i became. one of the most real games i have ever played. no game comes remotely close to being as immersive or having characters that feel so genuinely alive. and the people who tossed this game to the side after a few crashes and never gave it a second chance, don't deserve the masterpiece that it is now. legit a 10/10 game.

Awesome game, a bit misunderstood and overhated at launch.

Graphics are incredible overall, especially after all the updates. Night City is one of the most detailed and immersive open worlds ever made, full of life, neon lights, crowded streets and beautiful environments. Character models and facial animations are excellent, and the combat animations feel fluid and satisfying. Some NPC behaviors can still feel a bit awkward at times, but the overall presentation is top tier.

Sound design is amazing. Weapons sound powerful, cars feel aggressive, the city ambience is immersive, and the voice acting is top tier across the board. The music is absolutely phenomenal, perfectly matching every moment, whether you're cruising through the city at night, fighting through chaos, or experiencing emotional story moments.

Gameplay is great and highly engaging. Let's start with the combat, because many people still think Cyberpunk is just "GTA with RPG elements", but it offers so much more than that. There are many different playstyles to choose from: stealth hacking, aggressive gunplay, melee combat, katanas, throwing knives, cyberware-focused builds, snipers, shotguns, netrunning, and more. Every build feels different and has strengths and weaknesses depending on your approach and preparation. Combat is fast, fluid and rewarding, especially once your build starts coming together. The movement also feels great, especially with double jumps, dashes and cyberware upgrades that make you feel increasingly powerful.
The progression system is great, allowing you to specialize heavily into your preferred playstyle. Skill trees offer meaningful upgrades that genuinely change how you approach combat and exploration. Cyberware customization is one of the best parts of the game, letting you turn your character into an unstoppable machine, stealth hacker, samurai or whatever style you prefer.
Looting and equipment are also very satisfying. Finding iconic weapons, upgrading cyberware and experimenting with builds keeps the gameplay fresh for dozens of hours.
Exploration is another huge strength. Night City feels alive and dense, with hidden events, side gigs, random encounters and environmental storytelling everywhere. Even just driving around the city at night can be immersive on its own.
The side content is surprisingly strong too. Many side quests are written with the same quality as the main story, often presenting difficult choices, morally gray situations and memorable characters. Some side quests end up being more emotionally impactful than entire main campaigns in other games.
Another great mechanic is how your choices and relationships affect quest outcomes, dialogue and endings. Some opportunities, conversations and endings are only available depending on how you played and who you connected with throughout the game.
These are only some of the great mechanics the game offers, the rest are for you to discover.

The enemies are varied and fun to fight. Different gangs use different weapons, tactics and cyberware, forcing you to adapt your approach. Boss fights and cyberpsychos can also be very memorable, especially when your build is tested properly.

The story is excellent, emotional and extremely well written. The characters are memorable, believable and full of personality. V and Johnny Silverhand have fantastic chemistry throughout the game, and many side characters leave a strong impression too. The role playing aspects are very well done, with meaningful dialogue choices, different character backgrounds and multiple endings depending on your decisions. The game constantly presents morally complex situations where there is rarely a completely right or wrong answer. Also some of the endings are brutal, so be prepared to a rollercoaster of emotions.

In short, awesome game, a bit misunderstood because of its disastrous launch. After years of updates and improvements, the game became what people originally hoped it would be. Many people still judge it based on old impressions without giving it another chance. If you enjoy immersive RPGs, great storytelling, strong world building and flexible character progression, this game is absolutely worth experiencing.

I love everything about this game.  
From the insane cinematic graphics to the huge mod support, Cyberpunk has become one of my favourite games ever.

The optimisation is genuinely impressive it’s one of my go‑to titles for benchmarking whenever I overclock. Smooth performance, great scaling, and it always looks incredible no matter what settings I push.

The story is strong, the world is atmospheric, and the game has evolved massively since launch.
And yes it’s even better with mods. They take an already amazing experience and push it even further.

I love what this game has become. Night City is something special.
great gunplay and support for variety of action playstyles. Tons of great additions through free updates as well
Accurate representation of what it's like to live in California

This game is a solid 9.0/10 for me. I was expecting an almost Hollywood-level sugar-coated narrative you would see in generic action flicks. To my surprise, it made me really sad that V lives in a world with choices and trade-offs that come to a bitter end, simulating reality in that regard really well. The surrounding characters are also very well written and never stick out one bit in the plot at all, all their personalities resonate well with what they deal with or what they will deal with as the plot goes on. I couldn't get past completing more than two endings because I would just get grief. This game really captured what it's like living in a big city where everyone is a blip in a large population. It also captured what people always say about good stories in games that I've never seen in the usual genres i play until the point i felt empathetic with how everything transpires.

The gameplay is good, especially for an RPG. It has the most action-packed combat depth compared to the limited selection of RPGs I have played. There is some jank that isn't game-breaking, although it still requires some TLC. No, thanks to it being a rush job, I'll hedge my bets that needing to be a console release made it very hard to optimize as well. I heard from multiple people that CD Projekt Red cared enough about their product to fix it instead of shilling it to a community of fans that become both nostalgic and jaded over time because they have to fix it themselves to then make it playable like classic fallout games.

The graphics are good, nothing out of this world, especially in the case of the rendered models of the characters, they pale in comparison to the world around them. Where it does ramp it up is how the developers really captured the claustrophobic layout of cities, with buildings forming small bazaars on street levels. The buildings themselves had a lot of signs, pipes and other small details caked on them for that extra pop giving u the stimulation from city settings u would picture irl, and the detritus all around the city adds to the world-building really well. I would say it's the best modern city layout I've seen in an open world game yet, with the added flair of a futuristic cyber punk aesthetic.

TLDR: Having never played it before this, I don't have bad memories of a broken and unplayable game. Instead I was able to go in almost completely blind and enjoyed the game a lot.

The game actually surprised me quite a bit, as I was expecting a bland or shallow game (entirely due to residual internet disdain that I was exposed to), but in truth the story was great and had a lot of fun moments. The actual gameplay is really fun and more dynamic than something like Skyrim or Fallout, which are the closest games I've played to this sort of thing. Mixing fast movements with quickhacks and fun abilities makes this game really good in and out of combat. Katanas especially... those felt really fun to parry bullets with.

The driving is SO fun in this game, which is good because they love to do sidequests that involve a LOT of back and forth. Even playing on the standard difficulty I felt super overpowered for most of the game, which resulting in clearing enemy hideouts really quickly. That almost meant I spent even more time driving, which I was able to enjoy. It's especially fun when you save up enough money to get a car with tons of health and built-in machine guns, purely to cause mischief.

The game has some flaws here and there, but they're pretty minor gripes. Most of them involve slow elevators, and the fact that your character flashes you every time you change gear. Outside of that, I enjoyed the game enough that I immediately went and watched Cyberpunk Edgerunners after finishing this game, and was sad when there weren't any more quests to do. Highly recommend this if your computer has the specs to run it.

Everyone would agree it would be terrible to live in a world like this, and yet it seems like this is exactly what we're heading towards.

The game is far from perfect, after all the fix patches, there're still bugs in the game, they're not game breakers but they are there. Cant wait for the sequel though!

This game has come so far from its release debacle. With the bugs fixed and game optimization you can focus on the story, gameplay, upgrades, etc, if you love roleplaying story driven games this is the holy grail.

Few games in history have undergone a redemption arc quite like Cyberpunk 2077. While its initial launch was famously rocky, CD Projekt RED’s unwavering commitment to their vision—culminating in the massive 2.0 update and the brilliant Phantom Liberty expansion—has officially transformed this game into a certified masterpiece.

Night City is the undisputed star of the show. It is a towering, neon-soaked vertical labyrinth that feels genuinely alive, dangerous, and intoxicating. Walking through the crowded streets of Watson or driving through the desolate Badlands offers an unparalleled level of environmental immersion. The world-building is incredibly dense, capturing the corporate-dominated, gritty essence of classic cyberpunk lore.

It is impossible to talk about the renaissance of this game without mentioning Cyberpunk: Edgerunners, the phenomenal Studio Trigger anime series. Edgerunners didn't just capture the hearts of fans; it perfectly mirrored the game's high-stakes, tragic, and adrenaline-fueled atmosphere.

The anime injected a massive wave of fresh energy into the game, making the exploration of Night City feel even more personal. Visiting locations featured in the show, listening to "I Really Want to Stay at Your House" on the in-game radio, or equipping David Martinez’s iconic jacket adds a profound layer of emotional depth for anyone who loved the series.

With the overhauled cyberware systems, revamped skill trees, and chaotic vehicle combat introduced in recent updates, the gameplay finally matches the narrative's ambition. Combat is fast, brutal, and highly customizable. Whether you want to be a stealthy Netrunner hacking enemies from afar or a sandevistan-powered solo slicing through corporate guards with a katana, the playstyles are incredibly rewarding.

At its core, the story of V and Johnny Silverhand is a deeply moving tale about legacy, identity, and survival against all odds.

Cyberpunk 2077 is no longer just a game about promises; it is a triumphant delivery of an unforgettable sci-fi RPG. If you were waiting for the right time to lose yourself in Night City—especially if you are a fan of Cyberpunk: Edgerunners—there has never been a better time to chrome up and dive in.

There are so many things I like about this game, but the biggest is that it makes me feel better about myself as a software developer. Stacked objects will randomly fling themselves around, especially tires. Traffic randomly stops or collide, and vehicles tear off for no reason. Bodies get stretched or thrown off into the far distance. It's fun to jump into a beautiful cityscape and wreak havoc, constantly being reminded that it's just a game.
For the actual game, I'd give it a B+ these five years after its launch. It's far, far too cutscene driven. The main storyline is just small side quests between long cutscenes. I constantly press 'skip forward' to advance dialog. I've completed all side jobs and quests, gotten all of the rewards from the fixers, and completed 3 of 4 game endings, but what I really remember are the early story arcs and the feeling that I didn't really know which subplot to follow (Johnny? Takemura? Panam? Evelyn? Judy? Rogue? River?).
In that way it was like Elden Ring to me. Confusing, fun, bewildering, and eventually something I'm glad I mastered. Night City is a fun place to visit, but I wouldn't want to live there.

Romance in other games: Let's spend time together on a date.

Romance in Cyberpunk: Let's commit domestic terrorism!

best game ever with my gaming laptop rtx 4050 can handle 120-140 fps with dlss on performance high setting and 1 sharp. on quality dlss u will get like 90-100 fps - with ray tracing on and high mid settings u will get 60-70 fps dlss performance or quality. the optimization is just amazing, and the whole story and side quests are really really amazing.

This is genuinely one of the best games i have ever played. there are three main runs with the operating systems. i have beaten the game 3 times and have tried all the operating systems out and my personal favorite is the cyberdeck. you may only have 3 operating systems but the builds you can make with all the other cyberware and wepons give you near endless builds and insanly good replayability. if you are looking at this game because you watched the anime get it. if you are just looking at it or your friends suggested it, get it. whatever the reason is that you're looking at it, get it. i have a very budget PC. i mean 16gb of RAM and a GTX 3060 and i still run medium to high graphics with 60 fps. Turn on DLSS or somthing like that, set your settings and depending on your settings go with quality, balanced, or preformance (i run on quality) and you will have the best time of your life playing this game. Always bear iron and have a blast chooms!

Cyberpunk 2077 was an amazing experience from start to finish. Its story hooked me right away and completely immersed me in its world. I don’t usually play games in this genre, but Cyberpunk 2077 made me appreciate it and even become interested in it.

What surprised me the most was that after finishing the game, I thought no other game could make me feel the same way again. Then I played Phantom Liberty, and it gave me that experience all over again. It was every bit as good as the base game, and I can understand why some people even consider it better.

as for the game itself, i think i will give it 8/10,
PROS:
- It's fun to run around as Netrunner in Night City, but after several hours i kinda feel that the game is kinda too easy, maybe my decision making a Netrunner V is have a big role on it too.

- The world itself feels big and lively (even at low crowd settings),
 
- The story is good enough to keep me playing, especially Phantom Liberty DLC part, i think the Relic mechanic it introduce is really fun, and Tower Ending (without Spoiler) might be the best ending of all time in my opinion. I would say though, after you watch some in-game news on tv, you probably will be like me and think "wow, the world building in this game is kinda fun and chaotic".

- CDPR also have make mod support for the games, making it kinda easy to add mods, especially for stuff like tiny immersion fixes, you know, the unnecessary detail like making all in game ads fully animated or pettable cat (yes you can adopt cat and iguana in this game, weirdly enough, you can only pat the iguana, still, they are both cute though).

- also, the music are awesome, if you have watched Edgerunner on Netflix, you might already have taste a little bit of those juicy bangers. but let me tell you, most of the songs in this games are really good. Recently, i even start to make a habbit of listening to some of the songs while doing chores, especially Chippin In, Pon Pon Sh*t (Jhonny Silverhand version, it's easter egg, not in Radio) and Never Fade Away, they really make me forgot how exhausting doing daily laundry was. (FYI, most of the songs from anime are in Body Heat Radio btw, including that song).

- Easter eggs, lots of easter eggs, really fun. I kinda don't want to talk too much about this because it was really fun if you notice something you don't know, but just you know, there are a lot of eater egg in this game. (just so you know, Murkman is the best detective)

CONS:
-Even though most of annoying bug from first released was already been patched and fixed, there are still some minor bug or glitches in the game, Some like driving drift, texture pop-in (not as bad as first released) and that one time Jackie walk through lockers during the first mission are might tied to FPS and you machine, but stuff like auto drive feature driver recklesly or showing negative million numbers (it was without mod btw) and monowire doesn't glow at all sometimes are still small stuff that need fixes in my opinion. (also, i know Fream gen is bad since it will give you low latency, but so far, i can't use frame gen at all. It will crash on me when i open map, even when using the settings for RTX 30s and 20s, so i don't know what kind of stuff that will happened if you able to use it on RTX 20s or 30s).

- lack of consequences might be one factor that make the story can feel a bit monotone and less exciting for me. Like, don't get me wrong, some dialogue choice does lock you out from good reward, like when i miss my chance to get Jackie guns for free, but like what i just said, "for free", mean you don't really missing out much when you make a wrong choices, if you own the DLC you can just buy your best friend's gun from the vendor in Dogtown. pretty neat since it was your best friend's gun, you know something that supposed to be sentimental for his family and people close by :). And not only that, if you fail to save someone who will give you car if you save them, you can also just,,, buy the car. Yeah, no sh*t i actually kinda lost excitement when i find out i can just buy stuff i missed out, like, i might get that some stuff like buying car from someone being killed in street race kinda make sense, but buying your own best friend's gun? I don't think Mama Wells or Misty will appreciate me to buy item that so sentimental for them (it kinda make me feels like paying people to make myself a grave robber). And it's just about rewards, you can try to just never talk when the timer is on during dialogue, there are some exception that instant kill you, but most of them at least from my experience, you don't really need to do anything to resolve the situation. I know that CDPR doesn't advertise this game as RPG anymore since the only RPG you get from this game is only a character building game like most J-RPG, but i mean, even as action game, it kinda lost to GTA, i still remember how hard it was to make decision in GTA 4. Still, i appreciate that the game locked you out from the side mission if you fail to do it (even if you can just save scum it.

- on last thing that less  of annoying and more of nitpicking for me is that you can't save car you stole. I know we don't play as literal criminal and mor of a merc in need of help in this game, but that doesn't mean you can't be criminal in the game. I mean, since 2.0 update, they add wanted system, it was fun even though you can kinda cheese it with going to safe zone. Why not try making a mechanic where you can hack a system of the car and it to your collection? Like limited amount of stolen cars that can be save  to your cloud so you can call it whenever until it was blown up and make it so that stolen car can't be repaint using the crystalcoat because unlike El Capitan, when you break the system of the car you can restore the neat content inside it. Idk, having permanent different colors of the same cars kinda sound interesting and stealing car outside the quests feel kinda useless for me right now.

THINGS THAT I WANT IN THE FUTURE:
- more updates, even sometimes update can break the mods, some stuff does need to be fix imo
- more DLC, we will get Witche 3 DLC in 2026-2027 (probably), i don't think one DLC is enough for this game, Phantom Liberty was fun, but i hope it get more story contents, and i do think it deserve more, maybe stuff that connected to Project orion?
- This one is for Project Orion i think, in the next game i hope the mission will be much more harder, the choices have much more weight on it and at the start of the mission, you can get a choice to either hire a merc to help you or call you online friends to help you if they really do want to make add the online function, just for immersion, because doing the mission alone against crowded enemy that have so many chrome kinda feels weird, well, this one doesn't feel weird because of the plot, but i don't think the same plot can work twice (well, even if i given choice to go with people, i might go alone though since it's video game, lol)

runs smoothly with 2x frame gen, no delay to any controls.
good game if you wanna do anything and everything
wanna be unstoppable and kill everything
wanna be like quicksilver in that one scene in that one x-men movie you can

Cyberpunk 2077 is one of the most rewarding gaming experiences I’ve ever had.

I’ve been here since launch, and yeah, the beginning was rough. Bugs, performance issues, missing features, it deserved the criticism it got. But even back then, I could see the vision CD Projekt Red was aiming for, and I stuck with it because there was something special underneath all the chaos.

Then 2.0 and Phantom Liberty dropped, and I completely fell in love with the game all over again.

Phantom Liberty felt like a Bond movie thrown into the violent world of 2077. The espionage, the characters, the music, the tension is incredible. Combined with the massive gameplay improvements from 2.0, the game finally became what I always hoped it could be. Especially since 2.0 came out after the disappointment that was Starfield.

Night City is honestly one of the best worlds ever created in gaming. It’s visually stunning, dense, atmospheric, and a dystopian outlook on the future that feels like what were heading towards IRL. I constantly found myself getting lost in the streets, neon lights, and just trying to explore every hidden nook and cranny. I just couldn’t get enough of this game.

For me, Cyberpunk 2077 is a 10/10 game that, like a phoenix rose from the ashes, and is now one of my all-time favorite RPGs. The 100% grind is fun and rewarding with none of it feels like too much of a grind. If you haven't played it yet, please do.

I have mixed feelings about this game but I decided to give it a positive review because I don't think it's bad but it's not great either. I had fun playing it but I expected much more. Here are some of my thoughts:

- The graphics are beautiful and I love the vibe of this game. The open world looks impressive, but when you try to explore it, you quickly discover that most buildings are inaccessible and few places are interactive.

- Unfortunately, I couldn't fully immerse myself in the main story. Dialogue options are very limited and only give you an illusion of actually influencing the story. I'm used to RPGs giving me a lot of options on how I want the story to progress so I was a bit disappointed. Cyberpunk reminds me more of games like Far Cry than typical RPGs and I guess I expected a different experience. I feel like it's more of a shooter than an RPG.

- Cutscenes and some quests can be very tiring and feel slow because in most of them you just listen to a character talk and follow them around or sit in a car with them.

- The game tries to create a sense of urgency but in reality there's no time limit and you can complete as many side quests as you want without affecting anything. Even missions that start with a character calling you and saying they'd like to meet you at a certain time today have no time limit and you can delay them for as long as you want.

- The romance is okay but a lot of things get repetitive and feel a bit mechanical. I romanced Judy and every time she visited my apartment, she said the exact same thing, no new dialogue appeared and nothing interesting happened. She was just there and that's it.

- I'm not a huge fan of Johnny Silverhand. I like the idea of his character but I feel like his potential wasn't fully realized and his relationship with V could have been more interesting. It was a bit strange to me how he suddenly went from being hostile towards V to being nice. Also, Keanu Reeves' performance in this role felt flat and emotionless at times.

- I dread to think how bad this game was when it first came out because even after all these years it's still very buggy. Disappearing NPCs, items getting stuck in NPCs' hands, messy AI that causes enemies to randomly pause during fights and allow you to kill them easily, enemies seeing you through walls, NPCs bugging so you're unable to talk to them when you want to start/finish a quest, NPCs talking with their mouth closed, identical NPCs spawning in the same place, cars crashing into each other and exploding when you're not looking at the road (sometimes it looked like one car spawned on top of another and exploded), V picking up the phone, saying she can't talk right now and then immediately calling the person back, and many, many more.

- I've seen a lot of people complaining about the game's poor performance, but in my case it wasn't that bad and mostly ran smoothly even though my computer isn't high-end. It randomly crashed twice and that's it. Perhaps I just got lucky.

Overall, I'd say it's a pretty fun game but it does have a lot of flaws and I definitely recommend buying it at a discount. Maybe if I didn't have such high expectations because so many people consider this game to be one of the best, I wouldn't be so critical.

Still some minor bugs and what not overall, but It's ten times better than what it was years ago. It runs pretty great on my machine and it's still crazy (to me) to be able to run and play CP2077 and really immerse myself within the atmosphere of Night City...

You can always get mods to improve the performance and experience of your copy but overall out of the "digital box" it works more than well...

I could see myself getting a lot of hours within this game off and on. 

Plus if you really like Cyberpunk: Edgerunners, you'll REALLY love Cyberpunk 2077...!

I don't really have much else to say, this was meant to be brief. 

If you want a deeper review on Cyberpunk 2077 post update, then go watch your favorite 
Youtuber or something...

MY SCORE: 7.9/10 - 8.2/10

Cyberpunk 2077 might have the greatest gaming comeback.

When the game launched, it was basically a bug simulator with some emotional damage included for free. Players were crashing more than their in-game cars, and CD Projekt Red got cooked by the entire internet.

But instead of giving up and disappearing into the shadows like a stealth build, the developers kept grinding. Update after update, they fixed bugs, improved gameplay systems, reworked mechanics, and finally delivered the experience people were originally hyped for. Then came Cyberpunk 2077: Phantom Liberty, which honestly felt like the developers saying: “Alright, let us cook one more time.”

And somehow… they actually did.

Now Night City feels alive, polished, and insanely immersive. You can genuinely tell the devs cared about the game and refused to let it die. That kind of dedication is rare nowadays.

Cyberpunk 2077 went from being the internet’s favorite joke to one of the best RPG experiences of the last years and honestly, that redemption arc deserves respect.

Thank you CDPR, and never stop fighting.

Cyberpunk is the only long game I’ve ever fully completed, and I can already say with confidence that it’s one of the best games I’ve ever played.
For me, the world of Night City became something much bigger than just a game. It’s the atmosphere, the characters, the music, the dialogue, and the emotions that stay with you long after the credits roll.

During my first playthrough, there were so many moments that genuinely hit me hard - from pure excitement to the emptiness you feel after the ending. And those are the kinds of games that stay with you forever.
Cyberpunk 2077 gave me more than just a story - it gave me a feeling I’ll want to come back to again and again.

And I know for sure this won’t be my last playthrough.

I stayed away from this game for five years due to how bad the launch was, and after it being in my library for the past six months I decided to give it a shot.

This game is phenomenal. I have not felt this way about a game since New Vegas and I do not say that lightly. From start to end this game had me hooked, immersed and coming back for more. The gameplay loop is engaging and always adding a new spin to it, keeping the experience fresh. The world is immersive yet in a way that isn't merely a flashy spectacle that doesn't take away from the core gameplay. The characters are top notch and well written, so even if I don't agree or like what a character is doing I can completely understand the thought process and decisions they make.

If you were like me and steered clear of this game because of the launch and its horrible performance, I highly recommend you play this game, you won't regret it.

Cyberpunk 2077 is an incredibly good game, and on a strong system it performs very well while looking visually outstanding. Night City is one of the most detailed and atmospheric open worlds I have played, with its lighting, reflections, city design, and overall presentation making it feel genuinely alive.

What really pushed the visuals even further for me was the **RenoDX HDR mod**. With RenoDX, the game looks absolutely stunning. The HDR feels far more impactful, with deeper contrast, brighter highlights, and a much more cinematic image overall. Neon signs, dark streets, interiors, car lights, and the skyline all look incredible, especially on an OLED display.

The game itself is also very engaging. The story, characters, side missions, and overall world-building all do a great job of keeping you invested. It feels like a game that rewards you for taking your time, exploring, and actually paying attention to the world around you.

Performance was also very good in my experience, especially considering how visually demanding the game can be. With the right settings and setup, it delivers a smooth and impressive experience without taking away from the visuals.

Overall, *Cyberpunk 2077* is absolutely worth playing, especially now with the improvements it has received over time. If you have a good HDR display, I would highly recommend trying it with RenoDX, because it makes an already beautiful game look even better.

At launch it was a bit bumpy so I understand why it was talked badly about, however how it is now I would put it up as one of the greatest games I've ever played along with Divinity original sin 2 and Elden ring. it is such an amazing game that I cant really point out any one thing that makes it incredible because everything is, of course there's still some bugs as any game has but in my current 70 hour playthrough I've only run into a few and they were very minor. I would highly recommend playing this game to anyone because you can do whatever you want for 90% of the game and still feel good about it.

That's an amazing and absolutely unforgettable game.

Cyberpunk 2077 is all about the process. You can spend a few hours in Night City and not even notice how much time has passed. You WANT to explore. Maybe it's just me, but I genuinely wanted to look into every district, discover every possible thing, and just live in that city for a while. Night City feels believable — and at some point, you stop feeling like a player and start feeling like a person living there.

This game has an insane amount of content. There’s always something to do. Gigs, side jobs, random encounters, characters, hidden stories — at times it feels endless. And honestly, trying to finish everything in one playthrough is almost impossible.

But then something interesting happens. When you finally start running out of things to do, the city begins to feel strangely empty. Almost confusing. Like, “come on… this is Night City, there should always be something happening.” And somehow, that feeling fits the game perfectly.

Of course, the game isn’t perfect. The biggest issue for me was the bugs. There are still plenty of them — sometimes small, sometimes really noticeable. Occasionally they can completely break the immersion and make you laugh at a serious moment. Maybe I’m just too used to Rockstar-level polish, but I definitely felt that Cyberpunk could’ve been more refined in terms of quality of the open-world details and overall gameplay interactions, not just "a beautiful picture".

And of course — the ending. The characters are written incredibly well, so whichever path you take, it hits hard. For me, this was a story about change. Everyone has their own V, their own journey, and their own version of Night City.

I definitely recommend this game to anyone who hasn't played it yet.

And for everyone who already finished their journey in Night City — a tight embrace 🫂

P.S. — whoever was responsible for the soundtrack deserves their hands kissed. Some tracks hit harder than entire missions.

This is quite literally my favorite video game of all time. 

I love games like until dawn and life is strange that have butterfly effect choices that change how the story progresses. I love games like infamous that have action and great gameplay. Great writing, amazingly nuanced characters, fire soundtrack, extremely attentive game developers, fantastic voice acting, solid character design, immense respect for player customization… There’s a lot more I could say, but I’ll leave it at Cyberpunk 2077 is literally all of my favorite qualities of a video game put into one. Night City is also an absolutely beautiful city to explore (though I’m incredibly glad I don’t live there,) 

I didn’t know it existed at the time of launch and only played it for the first time in October of 2025, so I went in completely blind and just fell in love with the experience. It’s unlike anything I’ve ever experienced from a video game before in the best way possible. It also lowkey inspired me to get a pc and get into modding.

TLDR: I love and highly recommend this game. :)

It's been a while since I've played this game, but I figured I'd finally leave a review for it since one's been pending for a while.

This game was honestly always a good game. It may have had a rough start due to bugs and performance issues, but the core storyline and gameplay, as well as the more interesting questlines you can follow, were always there. Then with all the QoL changes and the complete overhaul they did to the perk system, it became much more refined and enjoyable to play since you now had a variety of builds and perks you could experiment with. 

I think one of my favorite things about the game was the story and characters. Every character has an interesting and expressive personality, including your own as expressed through the voice acting (I personally prefer Male V's voice direction). Everyone feels fleshed out and like they actually have a growing relationship to you the more you interact with them. The main story itself feels very personal and high stakes, though some of that is admittedly ruined by the open world nature of the game since you can just dally along however long you want. As the meme goes, "Anything but meeting Hanako at Embers" lol. Even so, I think it manages to portray a nice drama about the meaningfulness of life, self-determination, and even hope in a world that seems determined to grind you down.

The environment is effective in portraying just how degenerate and trashy (literally, even) Night City really is, which helps in creating this sense of "contamination" and a desire to separate yourself and rise above it. 

Also, the music. The music in this game kicks ass, and I still listen to some of my favorite tracks on my Spotify every so often.

I'd say my only real complaint with the game is the lack of being able to clearly show yourself as cybernetically enhanced, such as by having clearly mechanical limbs or metallic embellishments like a Kano-style red eye. I was also never too fond of any of the romance options, but it was nice that I never felt forced to pursue any of them from what I can remember.

I've tried to like this game but it just doesn't seem to keep my interest. Occasionally I'll pick it up for another go, but I soon find myself putting it down and playing something else to completion instead.

I'm sure it's purely a matter of personal taste as there aren’t many negatives that I can think of. At a push, the only two that come to mind are the complex controls (probably necessary as this is a big, complex game) and the fact that the setting isn’t very 'cyberpunk-y'.

That last point might be a generational thing, but I expect a dystopian cyberpunk city to be dark, choked with polution and so overcrowded that there’s hardly a gap between citizens.

I get that technical constraints have to be factored in though. If there’s a PC available today that could handle that many NPCs, then I certainly can’t afford it. 

Maybe CDPR could’ve REALLY leant into the smog angle and introduced some good old fashioned fogging to to reduce performance requirements?

Anyway, you may love this game as many others do, but it hasn’t really chimed with me.

One of my favourite stories in all of gaming 10/10, the DLC is 11/10 best stealth combat ive ever played.
Main stories combat is a bit janky but love the story makes up for it ten fold HF gonks :3

Cyberpunk 2077 is genuinely the best game I’ve ever played. Night City feels alive in a way no other open world does. Every street, building, and character has personality. The story is incredible, the soundtrack is perfect, and the characters actually make you care about them. Johnny Silverhand alone is more memorable than entire casts from other games.

What really makes the game special is the freedom. You can play as a stealth hacker, a cyber ninja, a walking tank, or some insane combination of all three. The combat feels smooth, the visuals are still some of the best in gaming, and the atmosphere is unmatched, especially at night with the neon lights and music blasting.

Yeah, the launch was rough, but after the updates and Phantom Liberty, this game became an absolute masterpiece. Most games are fun while you play them. Cyberpunk 2077 is a world you actually miss after you finish it.

Title: One of the best stories and characters I've experienced in gaming
Review:
Bought this on Steam and put in ~30 hours for my first playthrough. No regrets whatsoever.
The game had a rough launch years ago, but right now in 2026 it's absolutely worth it. The world of Night City is insane — detailed, alive, and full of personality. But what really carried it for me was the story and characters.
I went full romance with Panam Palmer and honestly... I caught real feelings by the end 😂 Her storyline is fantastic, and The Star ending gave me one of the most emotional and hopeful conclusions I've had in any game. Leaving Night City with the Aldecaldos and her just hit different.
Combat feels great once you get into it, the music and atmosphere are top tier, and the choices actually matter. Yes, there are still some bugs here and there, but nothing that ruined my experience.
If you like deep single-player RPGs with strong characters, meaningful choices, and a story that sticks with you after you finish — this is it. Especially if you take your time with side quests and romances.
Would I recommend? 100%. Already missing Panam and Night City.
Recommended (Very Positive)
the best game ever tons of graphics and very good story

Downpatching to the launch editions feels like opening a completly different game, with much more in depth skills, perks, and cyberware. the gunplay is a lot slower, much more tactical, which makes you really think about how you take fights.
Overall, love this game with all me heart

After 1.8 playthroughs of Cyberpunk, It is really, really, really good. The story is amazing. The worldbuilding is fantastic and the world is scraily close to what is happening right noe (- the incredibly cool looking cybernetics). It certainly exceeds the one dollar per hour guideline people set for video games. If you don't have the game, I highly suggest getting it, if 90 AUD isnt something you want to fork out for a game (Trust me I didn't think I'd want to either), Cyberpunk and its DLC, Phantom Liberty, both have steep discounts in Steam's seasonal discounts. Very fun and genuinely lifechanging.

Initially bought this game all the way back on pre-order, played it during the launch window, and the game ran horribly on my GTX 980 Ti.

But, the game also ran horribly on a lot of other PC with much better graphics card than mine at the time, so i stopped playing it when the game couldnt run properly on my GPU, and it also broke my GPU, by giving it the green screen of death, so i upgraded to a new system, currently attached. Runs smoothly now, except the Police system still seems to have bugs, and it still crashes my game after a few rounds of them in the same place. When to feedback to them on it, and seems to have no resolution even after they advise me on some mitigation steps to take.

Overall, a good game, but i am still traumatised by the initial launch. the game runs much smoother now after the 2.0 update, but, still has ways to go on the bug fixing, because an RTX 3080 should not be lagging in Dogtown.

Story: The main plotline of the game is simple and relatively short in its own right, but really begins to hold deeper meaning for the individual player as the world is explored beyond the main storyline. The DLC is great, the characters are great, and the small details of the world are excellent, leaving very few stones unturned when it comes to making the city feel like reality. Night City as a whole presents as a not-so-far off vision of the world in fifty to a hundred years if corporations triumph over government regulation entirely and become rulers in their own right. The average person in Night City lives uninsulated from violence, environmental ruin, and poverty, problems which have been exacerbated by the dominance of the billionaire class in America today.

Technical:

(spec: i9-14900 (non-K part), 4080 super FE on 566.36, 64gb ddr5, samsung 990 pro)

From my recollection, the game ran essentially flawlessly until about the 50 hour mark, where either some game data or cache issues began to show themselves, or I simply began to interact with elements of the game that somehow hadn't been as thoroughly debugged (although that doesn't make much sense). On this system in 4K, I was able to get an average of about 70fps throughout gameplay with pathtracing on as well as DLSS and FG and all graphics settings more or less maxed out. The one persistently problematic bug I encountered was a progressive drop in performance that would occur after booting the game, with FPS falling to the low single digits and getting stuck there, possibly causing a crash altogether or just requiring a restart of the game. Sometimes this glitch reoccurred almost immediately and was typically worsened by opening the menu, making me wonder if it is caused by some sort of memory leak or something else that could progress as the game runs. I never found a fix for this problem, but rebooting my PC altogether may have helped reduce the occurrence or time to failure somewhat.

Probably one of the best story games in the market, as for gameplay its an all rounder and mods just make the game better and better, still don't know why this game didn't get GOTY, heavily recommend trying this game, really fun.
Devs didn't optimize their game enough, runs like garbage.
